coredata.dc:title: Multimode interferometer based on no-core fiber with GQDs-PVA composite coating for relative humidity sensing
coredata.dc:creator: Tong, Rui-jie
Zhao, Yong
Chen, Mao-qing
Peng, Yun
coredata.dc:description: A simple fabrication multimode interferometer (MMI) with graphene quantum dots (GQDs) and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA) composite for relative humidity (RH) measurement is proposed and verified in this letter. The MMI includes two sections of single-mode fiber (SMF) and a length of 1.5 cm no-core fiber (NCF) is spliced between two sections of SMF, one of the splicing point is normal fused and the other splicing point is offset fusion splicing. The GQDs-PVA composite is prepared using a magnetic stirrer, and then is transferred to the surface of NCF by dip-coating machine. Refractive index of GQDs-PVA composite decreases with the increase of RH values caused by porous surface and water absorption characteristics of GQDs-PVA nanoparticle, which will lead to changes in the peak intensity of the interference spectrum. Experiment is executed at room temperature and the results indicate that the resonance intensity would decrease and the total intensity reduction of the interference spectrum was −20.473 dB with the measurement range changing from 38.8%RH to 88.65%RH. In addition, the proposed sensor presents good reversibility and repeatability. In a whole, the proposed sensor has simple structure, small size, low cost, high sensitivity and great repeatability.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Relative humidity (RH) sensor
Graphene quantum dots and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA)
Non-core fiber (NCF)

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure table body mmlmath acknowledge affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst primabst ref

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) Structure of GQDs, (b) Structure of GQDs of GQDs-PVA, (c) water molecules absorbed by GQDs-PVA composite.
The structure diagram of the sensor probe.
Spectrum of the sensor with different displacement.
(a) Normal melting point; (b) offset fusion splicing.
The preparation of GQDs-PVA and coating process.
GQDs-PVA composite: (a) just stirring evenly, (b) stand still for an hour, (c) Scanning electron microscopy of GQDs-PVA.
Spectrum of MMI with RH changing from 38.8 to 88.65%RH.
Relationship of intensity of interference valley with RH changing from 38.8 to 88.65%RH.
Transmission spectra variation with temperature changing from 21.2 °C to 39 °C and the linearity fitting of the sensor with the temperature changing from 21.2 °C to 39 °C.
Repetitive experiment of the RH sensor with RH changing from 38.8 to 88.65%.
Sensing performance comparison of different RH sensors.

head.ce:title: Multimode interferometer based on no-core fiber with GQDs-PVA composite coating for relative humidity sensing

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Information Science and Engineering, Northeastern University, Shenyang 110819, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Information Science and Engineering

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A simple fabrication multimode interferometer (MMI) with graphene quantum dots (GQDs) and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA) composite for relative humidity (RH) measurement is proposed and verified in this letter. The MMI includes two sections of single-mode fiber (SMF) and a length of 1.5 cm no-core fiber (NCF) is spliced between two sections of SMF, one of the splicing point is normal fused and the other splicing point is offset fusion splicing. The GQDs-PVA composite is prepared using a magnetic stirrer, and then is transferred to the surface of NCF by dip-coating machine. Refractive index of GQDs-PVA composite decreases with the increase of RH values caused by porous surface and water absorption characteristics of GQDs-PVA nanoparticle, which will lead to changes in the peak intensity of the interference spectrum. Experiment is executed at room temperature and the results indicate that the resonance intensity would decrease and the total intensity reduction of the interference spectrum was −20.473 dB with the measurement range changing from 38.8%RH to 88.65%RH. In addition, the proposed sensor presents good reversibility and repeatability. In a whole, the proposed sensor has simple structure, small size, low cost, high sensitivity and great repeatability.

ce:keyword.ce:text: Graphene quantum dots and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA)

ce:section.ce:para: The monitoring of relative humidity (RH) values in food processing, bio-pharming, grain storage and the precision instrument laboratory [1–4] is very essential. However, there are many disadvantages of the traditional electronic RH sensors applied in complex electromagnetic environment [5,6]. The service life of electrochemical RH sensors is affected by the use of environment and measurement accuracy will decrease when the RH is high for long time. Optical fiber sensor based on optical technology has proved to be feasible and has many advantages, such as not affected by electromagnetic and corrosion resistance, long distance sensing under bad environment and so on [7–9]. Fiber optic interferometers based on Mach–Zehnder, Fabry-Perot, FBG and Sagnac configurations have been reported [10–13]. Fiber RH sensors mainly rely on the optical fiber sensing structures coated with different RH sensitive materials to achieve RH measurement. The swelling and de-swelling effects process will affect the refractive index and thickness of the RH sensitive materials [14,15]. By this explanation, the RH sensitive materials combined with optical fiber sensing structures can realize RH measurement. At present, the commonly used RH sensitive materials include polycarbonate polymer, agarose, cellulose acetate, titanium dioxide, chitosan and polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) [16–21]. In 2004 year, Ainhoa Gastón [22] proposed a RH sensor with PVA coating on the side-polished single mode fiber, the sensitivity was 0.5 dB/%RH with the RH range of 70–90%RH. Although the sensitivity is high, the measurement range is too narrow and can’t meet practical application. In 2013, Li Xia and his groups [23] proposed a RH sensor with polymer coating on the surface of on no-core fiber, the sensitivity was 0.196 dB/%RH. While, the sensor only have good linearity when the RH is lower than 75%, which can’t meet the demand at high RH environment. In 2015, Haifeng Liu and Yinping Miao [24] put forward a RH sensor with SiO2 nanoparticles coating on S-Taper fiber, and the sensitivity, measurement range and the resolution are 0.441 dB/%RH, 83.8%RH −95.2%RH and 0.9945, respectively. However, the measurement range is too small and not appropriate for practical value. In 2016, Shengnan Wu [25] presented a Fabry-Perot (FP) interferometer (FPI) with PVA coated on the surface of hollow core fiber, and the sensitivity is up to −1.2 dB/%RH, while the measurement range is only from 86.5%RH to 90%RH. In 2017, Teng-hui and Ouyang [26] presented a RH sensor with MoSe2 coating on the side polished structure, and the sensitivity is 0.321 dB/%RH in the range of 32–73%RH, however, its testing linearity was not good, and the side polished fiber is fragile and influence their long term stability. It is well known that the sensitivity of RH optical fiber sensor is mainly depend on the swelling and de-swelling effects. Graphene with the characteristic of high surface/volume ratios appears to people’s eyes [27]. In the same year, Yaoming Huang [28] proposed an optical fiber RH sensor based D-shape coated with rGO, and the sensitivity is 0.427 dB/%RH with the RH changing from 58.2%RH to 92.5%RH, but side-polished fiber is too fragile that limits its application. A high sensitivity RH sensor with good linearity and wide measurement range is needed at present.

To solve the above problems, a multimode interferometer (MMI) based on non-core fiber (NCF) misplaced welding with single mode fiber (SMF) and coated with graphene quantum dots (GQDs) and polyvinyl alcohol (GQDs-PVA) composite was designed for RH monitoring. NCF acted as the sensing material carrier and generated multi-mode inspired by the lead in light. The chemical and physical properties of RH-sensitive film would be influenced as the RH change and affect the propagation mode of light in NCF. Finally, the output spectrum of the sensing probe shifted. Based on this interpretation, the relationship between RH and the spectra intensity could be determined. What’s more, the reversibility and repeatability experiments were made to characterize the performance of the proposed sensor. The good characterization of reversibility and repeatability proves that the sensor has good practical value.

Graphene quantum dots (GQDs), as a kind of graphene material, has many hydrophilic groups on the surface, which makes it good candidate for RH sensing. Carboxylate groups negatively charged on the surface of GQDs surface is easier to absorbed water molecules than the OH functionalities [29]. The adsorbed water molecules will decrease the density of electron on the surface of GQDs [30], and then cause the decrease of the refractive index, which can be used to modulate propagating mode in optical fiber cladding and realize sensing measurement.

However, easily dispersed in water of GQDs [31] makes it easy fall off from optical fiber used in a high RH environment for a long time, which limit the use of the GQDs in optical fiber sensor. PVA, as a polymer hygroscopic material, have a certain of stickiness and can combine well with optical fiber and has been applied for RH optical sensors [21], however, the nature of PVA itself limits the RH sensitivity. In addition, the refractive index and volume of PVA have the same changing trend with GQDs. What’s more, there are many hydroxyl, carboxyl and carbonyl on the surface of GQDs, the hydroxyl on the surface of GQDs makes it easy to combine with PVA. One of the most notable feature of GQDs-PVA composite film is hydrophilic, and more water molecules introduced by increasing RH values will be absorbed by GQDs-PVA composite. The structure of GQDs, GQDs-PVA and the water absorption process of the composite is shown in Fig. 1 . The sheet of GQDs layers and the spaces between PVA and GQDs layers will full of water molecules introduced by increasing RH values, which means the GQDs-PVA composite will swell and the refractive index of GQDs-PVA will decrease.

The structure of the proposed multimode interferometer (MMI) is schematically shown in Fig. 2 . The proposed sensing structure is fabricated by splicing a section of NCF between a core-offset fusion and a normal fusion makes. The core-offset fusion makes the incident in light easier to couple into the NCF, and the normal fusion make it easier for light to couple into the output SMF. Compared with traditional SMF, higher order modes and the stronger evanescent wave can occur when light enters into NCF without cladding, which is more sensitive to surrounding refractive index.

The multiple modes are excited after the light transferred from lead-in SMF to NCF, and then the light transferred along NCF before the second splicing point. As the optical fiber is cylindrical, which makes the input field is symmetrically and light propagating in NCF enters into the cladding and core of SMF. Assuming the LP0m described as
ϕ m
r
, and the field in NCF can be expressed as [32]:
(1)
E

m = 1

r
where M represents the number of modes in NCF; bm represents the coefficient of excitation and is defined as
(2)
b m
=

E
r , 0

d r

d r
When distance of light propagation is z, the filed distribution can be can be expressed as
(3)
E

m = 1

where propagation constant and the coefficient of evanescent absorption of LP0m mode can be expressed in βm and γ m , respectively, and γ m can be described as:
(4)
γ m

cs

where λ is the free space wavelength, aλ is coefficient of attenuation of the GQDs-PVA film at λ, the refractive indices of the GQDs-PVA film and NCF can be expressed in ncs and nncf , respectively, a is the NCF radius, the critical angle can be expressed in θc , and can be described as [32]:
(5)
θ m

sin
- 1
β m
k
where k = 2πnncf /λ is number of interference wave. By overlap integrating E(r,z) and Es (r), the transmission of interference spectrum can be obtained and described as:
(6)
T = 10
log 10
∫ 0 ∞
E s

E

E
r , z
2
r d r
where Es (r) represents the fundamental mode of lead-out SMF. As the increase of RH, ncs and aλ represent the refractive index and coefficient of GQDs-PVA, respectively. βm and γm are the propagation constant and coefficient of evanescent absorption, respectively, and are affected by ncs and aλ . All the above variables affect the interference spectrum and can be described by formula (6). Therefore, the sensitivity can be quantified by detecting the movement of the spectral valley position.

Experimental system diagram of RH is shown in Fig. 3 , which includes an amplified spontaneous emission source (ASE) acting as light source, an optical spectrum (OSA) with spectral resolution of 0.02 nm acting as signal demodulator and the sensing structure. Five plastic bottles filled with saturated salt solutions were prepared and provided different RH values for RH sensing experiments. The hygrometer was used to calibrate the specific values in the plastic bottles and got power from the DC voltage source. For fabricating the sensing structure, a section of NCF (diameter-125 μm) with 10.47 mm was spliced between two sections of ordinary SMF (Corning, diameter-125 μm), and the splicing process was done by commercial fusion splicer (FITEL S178). One of the melting point was normal melting, and the other was offset fusion splicing. The interference spectrum was affected by the misplacement. Fig. 4 showed the interference spectrum with different misplacement, where the length of NCF is about 1.5 cm. In Fig. 4, we can see that the contrast of the spectrum is relatively small when the misplacement is more than 10.47 μm. While the interference spectrum isn’t obvious, which indicates it is most suitable for sensing when the misplacement is 10.47 μm. The specific details of melting point is shown in Fig. 5 , Fig. 5(a) was normal melting point and Fig. 5(b) was offset fusion splicing, and the misplacement was 10.47 μm. In addition, the coating thickness in the structure will affect the characteristics of the proposed sensor, which has been discussed in Ref. [6]. Therefore, the coating thickness in the structure is 2.48 μm in this experiment.

The GQDs-PVA making and coating process are shown in Fig. 6 : (1) Adding 127.9 mg PVA to the beaker firstly, and then 7 mg GQDs solution with the concentration of 1 mg/mL was added into the beaker slowly. (2) Stirring the mixture of PVA and GQDs with a magnetic stirrer for half an hour with the temperature of 30 °C to make sure the GQDs-PVA composite were well-mixed, and the mixture was shown in Fig. 7 (a), however, there were so many bubbles in it, which would introduce inhomogeneous coating and affect the sensing performance. In order to eliminate the effect of bubbles on the sensing performance, the GQDs-PVA composite were put at room temperature for one hour, and the GQDs-PVA composite without bubbles were shown in Fig. 7(b). The mixed results could be obtained by electron microscopy which was shown in Fig. 7(c). (3) adjusting the speed of drawing machine and coating GQDs-PVA composite on the sensing structure in the sensing structure by the dip coating machine; (4) In order to improve the binding force between GQDs-PVA and NCF, the sensor was put at 30 °C by natural wind dry method for 4 h. (5) After the above steps, a section of NCF uniformed coated with GQDs-PVA was obtained.

Before the experiment, the sensor probe was fixed on the bottle cap and kept it at the same horizontal position with RH meter to make sure the accuracy of RH values. Then the experiment exacted at room temperature and the RH-sensitive characteristic of MMI structure was studied. The spectrums of different RH values are seen in Fig. 8 , where the intensity of the resonance spectra decreases with the increase of RH and the total decrease of the intensity is −20.473 dB with the RH changing from 38.8%RH to 88.65%RH. Fig. 9 gives the tendency of the resonance intensity versus RH, where each resonance intensity shifts averaging −0.421 dB/%RH with the linear correlation coefficient of 99.876% in the measurement range changing from 38.8%RH to 88.65%RH, and the good linearity indicates the sensor can have a wider measurement range. In addition, the error bars of the experimental data is showed in Fig. 9, where it shows the output result of the proposed sensor for each RH point changes in a certain range. Compared with the actual value, the maximum drift of the measured value is −0.3882 dB, which is mainly caused by the interference of surrounding temperature, hygrometer reading error. Therefore, the property of spectrum variation with temperature has been studied. The different temperature values are supplied by a temperature controlled oven, and the change of spectrum with temperature is shown in Fig. 10 , which indicates the temperature sensitivity of the sensor is 0.16 dB/°C with the R2 = 0.984 in the measurement range changing from 21.2 °C to 39 °C. The result of temperature test illustrates RH sensing characteristics will be affected by temperature. Therefore, the RH sensing process should be operated at room temperature consideration. If the RH sensor is to be used at other temperature values, a FBG can be cascaded with the proposed RH sensor to eliminate temperature crosstalk and realize the detection of RH and temperature at the same time, which has been proposed and experiment tested in our previous works [6]. In a whole point of the view, the experimental results reveal that the sensor has good performance.

Comparison with other optical RH sensors based on different hygroscopic materials is presented in Table 1 . From Table 1, we can find that Ref. [24], Ref. [34] and Ref. [24] have a higher sensitivity than the proposed sensor in this paper, however, the measurement range of Ref. [24] and Ref. [23] is far less than that of the proposed sensor. Although the measurement of Ref. [36] and the proposed sensor is quite similar, the sensitivity of Ref. [36] is non-line. In addition, the response time of the proposed RH sensor is mainly depend on the sensitive material. In Refs. [25,29], it has mentioned that the response time of GQDs and PVA to RH is less than 3 min, so the response time of the sensor isn’t tested in this paper.

However, sensitivity is not the only criterion to evaluate the performance of a sensor. Therefore, reversible and repeatable experiments are tested. Fig. 11 shows the reversibility and repeatability of the sensor, there are some slight measuring errors at each RH experiment point except at 76.34%RH, where the fluctuation is about 0.300 dB, which possibly results from the temperature change. Besides, out-sync of reading RH values and getting spectrum data also causes fluctuation, because, the resolution of the OSA is 0.02 nm, which means the fluctuation maybe resulted by read error. What’s more, temperature fluctuation may also result in the measuring error.

In addition, the selectivity is also essential in practical sensing applications. Considering the RH sensitive materials adopted in this letter, the change of gas content should be considered. While, Z.S. Hosseini and his groups [29] have proposed an electric RH sensor based on GQDs and discussed the influences of the change of gas content on the sensing performance, the gas includes CO, H2, CH4, and CO2. The results showed that the sensor responded weakly to H2, and there was no considerable response to the other tested gases. What’s more, PVA, as a crosslinking agent, isn’t sensitive to the gases mentioned above.

In conclusion, a multimode interferometer with GQDs-PVA coated on the surface of no-core fiber for RH sensing is proposed and experimentally verification. The interferometer is constructed by splicing a section of NCF between two sections of single mode fiber, one of the splicing point is core-offset fusion, and the other splicing point is normal fusion. The optimum sensing length of no-core fiber is 1.5 cm that has been discussed in this paper, which illustrates the proposed sensor is small size. Due to the tunable refractive index of the GQDs-PVA, the RH changing can be monitored by recording the intensity changes of interference valley, the sensitivity can be obtained and is 0.421 dB/%RH with the RH range changing from 38.8%RH to 88.65%RH, and the linear correlation coefficient is 99.876%. In addition, reversibility and repeatability of the proposed RH sensors are investigated and experiments show good performance. What’s more, the affection of temperature on sensor performance is also studied, and the temperature sensitivity is 0.16 dB/°C, which indicates that temperature would affect RH sensing performance. In practical process, the temperature crosstalk could be eliminated by cascaded a FBG, which has been adopted in our previous work [6]. In addition, the comparison with other RH sensors indicates the proposed sensor has wide measurement range and high sensitivity. Reversible and repeatable experiment indicate the proposed sensor has good reversibility and great repeatability. In a whole, the small size, high sensitivity, good reversibility and reversibility indicate the proposed sensor has application prospects.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported in part by the National Natural Science Foundation of China under Grant 61425003 and 61773102, the Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities under Grant N160408001, and part by the State Key Laboratory of Synthetical Automation for Process Industries under Grant 2013ZCX09.
